blocks.content: Twelve steps, banister loose but present. Kit went first, testing each step on the outer edge, pointing to soft spots without turning around. Third step. Eighth. Eleventh.
At the top: a hallway, four doors. Light came through a window at the far end where a pane had gone missing.
Eli and Savannah took the first door. Kit and Daisy went to the end of the hall.

It had been a child's room. Small bed frame, mattress gone, empty shelves. On the wall, still pinned: a crayon drawing on yellow construction paper. A house with smoke from the chimney. Two people outside it. A sun with a face.
Daisy stopped in the doorway. Kit stood beside her.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Hey — there's a whole bathroom in here still set up. Like, soap and everything. Bar soap in a dish.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: That might be the saddest thing in this house.
She looked at him. He looked at the drawing.
